description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its length. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The block is predominantly dark in color, with the grooved areas revealing a slightly lighter, bluish stone texture. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with no visible joints or separations, suggesting it is a single, monolithic piece. The overall appearance is robust and utilitarian, suitable for use as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. The surface finish appears slightly rough, providing traction and durability.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, alternating between dark and bluish stone. The block is solid, robust, and visually suited for use as an urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat face and the horizontal grooves running across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and vertical alignment of the grooves, emphasizing the striped pattern.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the horizontal grooves and the block's solid structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the vertical grooves and the pronounced striped effect.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
